coredata.dc:title: Sensor properties for Fe3+ and synthesis of new a bis –(3-(benzhydryloxy) propanoate) phthalocyaninato silicon (IV)
coredata.prism:copyright: © 2024 Elsevier B.V. All rights are reserved, including those for text and data mining, AI training, and similar technologies.
coredata.prism:publisher: Elsevier B.V. All rights are reserved, including those for text and data mining, AI training, and similar technologies.
coredata.dc:creator: Solğun, Derya Güngördü
Ağırtaş, Mehmet Salih
coredata.dc:description: In this study, 3-(benzhydryloxy) propanoic acid (2) and bis –(3-(benzhydryloxy) propanoate) phthalocyaninato silicon (IV) (3) compounds were synthesized. Their compound structures were characterized with the help of IR, UV–visible, mass and 1H NMR spectroscopy. Aggregation and solubility of the compounds were tested. Finally, the metal sensor property of the axial phthalocyanine compound was investigated against Cu2+, Fe3+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Na+, Zn2+, Ag+, Ba2+, Cd2+ ions. It was determined that the compound was selectively sensitive to Fe3+. The optical detection of Fe3+ ions in the presence of Cu2+, Fe3+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Na+, Zn2+, Ag+, Ba2+, Cd2+ ions shows that this material can be used for easy and practical analysis.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Metal sensor
Phthalocyanine
Absorption
Fe3+ ions
Selective

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: UV-Vis spectra of compound 3 in 4.8 × 10−6–2.4 × 10−6 M.
UV-Vis absorptions of compound 3 in different solvents.
UV–visible spectrum bands of compound 3 with different metal ions in DMF.
Diagram representation of the spectral data in Fig. 3.
Plot of UV–visible spectrum versus Fe3+ ion concentration.
Linear calibration curve of the study.
Synthesis of compound (2) and the axially phthalocyanine (3).

head.ce:title: Sensor properties for Fe3+ and synthesis of new a bis –(3-(benzhydryloxy) propanoate) phthalocyaninato silicon (IV)

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Chemistry, Faculty of Science, Van Yuzuncu Yıl University, Van 65080, Turkey

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Department of Chemistry, Faculty of Science, Van Yuzuncu Yıl University

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Department of Chemistry, Faculty of Science, Van Yuzuncu Yıl University, 65080, Van, Turkey

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: In this study, 3-(benzhydryloxy) propanoic acid (2) and bis –(3-(benzhydryloxy) propanoate) phthalocyaninato silicon (IV) (3) compounds were synthesized. Their compound structures were characterized with the help of IR, UV–visible, mass and 1H NMR spectroscopy. Aggregation and solubility of the compounds were tested. Finally, the metal sensor property of the axial phthalocyanine compound was investigated against Cu2+, Fe3+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Na+, Zn2+, Ag+, Ba2+, Cd2+ ions. It was determined that the compound was selectively sensitive to Fe3+. The optical detection of Fe3+ ions in the presence of Cu2+, Fe3+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Na+, Zn2+, Ag+, Ba2+, Cd2+ ions shows that this material can be used for easy and practical analysis.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Phthalocyanine compounds have been investigated for different applications since their first synthesis. Phthalocyanines are not found in nature [1]. However, it is obtained by synthesis [2]. This situation necessitates new designs in the synthesis of phthalocyanine compounds. The new synthesis derivatizations can undoubtedly exhibit more or less new properties. In this context, approximately 1200 articles on phthalocyanine compounds are published annually [3]. Phthalocyanine compounds are widely used in fields such as paint [4], optics [5], catalyst [6], liquid crystal [7], sensor [8,9], photosensitizer [10–12], hybrid material creation [13,14], electrochromic [15], solar cells [16,17]. It has also recently been investigated for its antioxidant, antifungal and antibacterial properties [18–20]. Phthalocyanines designed for all these usage potentials should be soluble and not show aggregation. For this reason, solubility enhancing groups are placed in axial, peripheral and non-peripheral positions to ensure solubility of phthalocyanine compounds [21]. At this point today, the world is getting polluted rapidly and it is known that especially water resources are vitally important. It is known that some pollutants (such as organic dyes, transition metal ions, nitroarenes and pesticides) cause environmental pollution as a result of rapid industrialization [22]. In order to keep water and environmental pollution under control, it must be monitored with continuous and effective tests. To this end, some chemo sensors are used to detect transition metals. Some noble metal nanoparticles loaded into microgels, metal-organic frameworks, and some organic cross-linked polymers are used for this purpose [23–25]. However, their expense necessitates the synthesis of cheaper chemo sensors. For this reason, designing phthalocyanine complexes as chemo sensors for this purpose will be an effective solution to solve this problem. In this way, effective compounds can be obtained for usage areas. Axial phthalocyanine complex was performed using substituents with solubility-improving effect. The selectivity of this phthalocyanine compound against Cu2+, Fe3+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Na+, Zn2+, Ag+, Ba2+, Cd2+ ions was investigated.

Diphenyl methanol, 3-chloropropionic acid, acetonitrile, CHCl3, CH2Cl2, ethyl acetate, DMF, THF, DMSO, K2CO3, ZnCl2, ethanol and methanol chemicals were used as purchased from Sigma, Aldrich, Merck or TCI companies. Organic solvents were used as received and in spectroscopic quality unless otherwise stated.

Stuart SMP3 equipment was used for melting points. In the structure characterization of the compounds, Agilent 400 MHz spectrometer was used for NMR, Thermo Scientific FT-IR spectrophotometer for functional group vibrations, Hitachi U-2900 Spectrophotometer for electronic transitions and also LC-MS electrospray ionization technique for mass.

1.8423 g diphenyl methanol and 1.0852 g of 3-chloropropionic acid were refluxed in 20 mL of toluene for 20 hours. Before the crude product formed, the solvent was removed with used a rotary evaporator. The product is soluble in THF, DMF, DMSO, CHCl3, CH2Cl2, acetonitrile, ethyl acetate, ethanol and methanol. M.P: 70–72 °C. Yield: 1.40 g (55 %), FTIR (ATR), ν/cm−1: 3390(OH), 3059 (Ar-H), 2920, 2850, 1735, 1598, 1597, 1494, 1446, 1350, 1269, 1170, 1078, 1016, 925, 910, 850, 752, 694. 1H NMR (400 MHz, DMSO-d6), (δ): 7.41, 7.40, 7.33, 7.22, 5.94, 5.74, 3.42, 2.51. 13C NMR (300 MHz, DMSO-d6) δ ppm: 146.21, 128.70, 127.18, 126.37, 74.59, 39.83. MS (ESI), (m/z): Calculated for C16H16O3: 256.1; Found: 279.10 [M+Na]+.

SiPcCl2 (75 mg, 0.12 mmol), 3-(benzhydryloxy) propanoic acid (122.9 mg, 0.48 mmol) were mixed with dry toluene (10 mL) and refluxed for 24 h. The solvent was removed when the temperature reached room temperature. THF solutes were taken. This compound dissolves in THF, chloroform, DMF, dichloromethane, DMSO, MeOH, EtOH. Yield: 25 mg (40 %), FTIR (ATR), ν/cm−1: 3387, 3084 (Ar-H), 2918, 2848, 1743, 1598, 1529,1494, 1446, 1402, 1334, 1269, 1170, 1120, 1080,1018, 912, 852, 825,732, 694. 1H NMR (400 MHz, DMSO-d6), (δ): 7.84, 7.38, 7.37, 7.31, 7.23, 7.21, 5.89, 5.69, 3.36, 2.51. UV–Vis (THF, 1×10–5 M): λmax/nm (log ε): 665 (5.16), 350 (4.72).

3-(benzhydryloxy) propanoic acid (2) was synthesized by the reaction of diphenyl methanol and 3-chloropropionic acid compounds. Then, SiPcCl2 was reacted with 3-(benzhydryloxy) propanoic acid in dry toluene to obtain the compound bis–(3-(benzhydryloxy) propanoate) phthalocyaninato silicon (IV) (3). The reaction routes of the obtained compounds are shown in Scheme 1.

IR, UV–visible, mass and NMR spectroscopy data were used for the characterization of the compounds. In the IR spectrum of compound 2, the OH group peaks at 3390 cm−1 and the carbonyl group at 1735 cm−1. CH2 groups give the expected characteristic peaks at 2920 and 2850 cm−1. CC peaks give peaks at 1598 and 1597 cm−1. C-O-C peak is observed at 1269 cm−1. These functional vibrations support the structure of the compound. The IR values for Compound 3 show the expected vibrations axially, except for minor shifts. These shifts, for example, occur at aromatic vibration (Ar-H) at 3084 cm−1, while CH2 vibrations can be observed at 2918 cm−1 and 2848 cm−1.
Aromatic protons of compound 2 are observed in the range of 7.41–7.22 ppm in the 1H NMR spectrum. CH proton is observed at 5.94 ppm. CH2 protons are observed at 3.42 and 2.51 ppm.

The peaks at 146.21, 128.70, 127.18, and 126.37 ppm observed in the aromatic region in the 13C NMR spectrum support the structure. In addition, peaks at 74.59 and 39.83 indicate CH and CH2. Aromatic protons of compound 3 are observed in the range of 7.84–7.21 ppm in the 1H NMR spectrum. CH proton is observed at 5.89 ppm. CH2 protons are observed at 3.36 and 2.51 ppm.

It is observed that the absorption value of compound 3 shown in Fig. 1 below does not aggregate in a certain concentration range. This is an indication that the compound has a suitable structure for different application areas. Phthalocyanine compounds must have non-aggregation behavior especially for photodynamic therapy [26]. At the same time, high solubility of phthalocyanine compound in water or other solvents is another important parameter desired. The absorption values in different solvents showing the solubility of the compound are shown in Fig. 2. Phthalocyanines have a strong absorption in the near-red region of the visible spectrum and the delocalized 18 π-electrons in their structure give them unique physical and chemical properties. These unique properties allow them to be used in fields such as optics, liquid crystal, catalyst, sensor, photodetector, pigment, gas sensor, electrochromic and electrocatalytic [27,28].

One of the tools for detecting selective features is UV–vis spectroscopy. As it is known, selective detection of metal ions accurately and in a short time is possible in this way. Practical methods of determination have always attracted the attention of researchers. Here, the sensitivity of compound 3 to Cu2+, Fe3+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Na+, Zn2+, Ag+, Ba2+, Cd2+ ions was investigated. Selective determination of Fe3+ ions is seen as a practical method for researchers to determine their absorption and emission spectra optically [29,30]. Studies are reported in the literature that phthalocyanine compounds act as sensors in the selective determination of Fe3+ ions [8,12,31]. Studies that synthesize carbon dots and use them as fluorescent probes to detect Fe3+ ions are also reported [32]. This study reveals that different phthalocyanine derivatives show similar selectivity. Here, the compound in question offers a more easily applicable method for Fe 3+ ion, which creates different environmental pollution. Optically detecting Fe3+ ions is undoubtedly more advantageous as an optical sensor selectively detecting it independently of the traditionally used extraction, oxidation and reduction methods. To determine the effect of compound 3, measurements were made both in its presence and absence. For this, various metal ions (Cu2+, Fe3+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Na+, Zn2+, Ag+, Ba2+, Cd2+) were prepared (
Figs. 3 and 4). A solution of metal ions in dimethyl formamide in compound 3 and (10−2 M) in (10−6 M) was prepared. A homogeneous solution was made by mixing. 3 mL and 10−6 M solution of compound 3 were taken into quartz cuvettes and 10 µL of each metal solution was added using a micropipette. For each, the corresponding UV–visible spectrum measurements were made and reported. Absorption band was observed in the spectrum at 350 and 665 nm due to the characteristic π-π* transition specific to compound 3. No change was observed in the spectrum in the presence of metal ions except Fe3+ ion. It shows that compound 3 is selective towards Fe3+ ions (
Figs. 5 and 6). Compound 3, which acts as a sensor for Fe3+ ion, is observed as a new and different absorption band in the UV–visible region. Some advantages of the fact that the presence of Fe3+ ions can be determined optically by UV absorption can be summarized as follows: Getting a response in a short time, being able to work at low concentrations, and being easily applied. With this behavior, this method is expected to provide a more practical use compared to existing methods.

Studies report a large number of cation sensors [33]. Especially considering the disadvantages of the probes and the design of ideal metal ion sensors, it stands out. The most effective criteria are standards such as high sensitivity, efficiency, simplicity, selectivity, and reliability. Thanks to these criteria, the sensors developed can show tremendous potential in the detection and analysis of a wide variety of metal ions such as K+, Fe2+/Fe3+, Ca2+, Al3+, Cr3+. Studies report that the chemosensory capacity of phthalocyanine compounds is not limited to metal ions and that they act as sensors against different chemical compounds (acetaminophen and picric acid) [34,35]. Although the number of concentration ranges considered in this study is higher than previously reported iron(III) sensors, the results are almost the same [8]. This shows that the study was conducted methodically appropriately. In a study conducted using the Absorbance and Fluorescence methods as sensors, it was reported that iron (III) ions were selectively detected among 14 ions [36]. In another study, Fe+3 ion was determined as selective among 15 metal ions [37]. Studies in which Fe+3 ions are optically determined among 19 metal ions are given in the literature [38]. Different chemicals are used for each of these studies. In this study, the use of a macrocyclic compound and its detection with an easy method such as Absorbance make the study more practical and applicable. This study shows that macromolecular structures such as phthalocyanine can be used as sensors for environments threatened by pollutants such as water and the environment.

In this study, 3-(benzhydryloxy) propanoic acid and bis–(3-(benzhydryloxy) propanoate) phthalocyaninato silicon (IV) (3) compounds were synthesized. Compounds were characterized by FT-IR, UV visibility, Mass and 1H NMR spectra. Solubility and aggregation tests of the phthalocyanine complex were performed. The selectivity of compound 3 against Cu2+, Fe3+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Na+, Zn2+, Ag+, Ba2+, Cd2+ions was investigated. It was determined that this compound has selectivity towards Fe3+ ions. Under the same conditions, it does not have any selectivity towards Cu2+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Na+, Zn2+, Ag+, Ba2+, Cd2+ ions. As a result, this compound can be determined optically by absorption spectroscopy as a practical and easy method for detecting Fe3+ ions. It is expected to be used as a practical method for detecting pollutants.

Mehmet Salih Ağırtaş: Writing – review & editing, Visualization, Validation, Supervision, Resources, Investigation, Formal analysis, Data curation, Conceptualization. Derya Güngördü Solğun: Visualization, Validation, Investigation, Formal analysis, Data curation, Conceptualization.
There is no conflict of interest between the authors.
No potential conflict of interest was reported by the authors.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.
